title: 10 best herbal teas for gum infection

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tree teas helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacterial growth and inflammation in the mouth. The tea's essential oils, particularly terpinen-4-ol, have been shown to reduce plaque and gingivitis, while also reducing pain and swelling associated with gum infections. Additionally, tea tree teas can help to strengthen gum tissue and promote healthy oral hygiene, making it a natural and effective remedy for addressing gum infection and preventing further complications.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Tea tree oil, which is often used to make tea tree teas, has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and fungal infections in the mouth, including those that cause gum disease.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in tea tree tea may help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infection, making it a natural remedy for reducing discomfort.
Antioxidant property: Antioxidants found in tea tree tea can help protect the mouth from further damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and disease progression.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the tea tree plant are used to make medicinal teas for gum infection due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and prevent bacterial growth.
Barks: The barks of the tea tree plant are rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help to dry up infected areas and reduce bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried melaleuca alternifolia leaves and steep them in one cup of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to infuse for five minutes then strain it into a clean cup.
Discard the solids and add two drops of peppermint essential oil for enhanced flavor and oral care benefits.
Let the tea cool down to room temperature before using it as a mouthwash or gargling liquid.
Use the tea within one hour after preparation and store any leftover in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with gum infection because it contains potent antibacterial compounds that effectively combat bacteria causing periodontitis. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling and ease pain, allowing for faster healing of damaged tissues. Additionally, the antioxidants present in Kansas coneflower tea help protect gums from further damage by neutralizing free radicals. By addressing these underlying causes, Kansas coneflower teas can be a valuable natural remedy for promoting healthy gums and preventing gum infection recurrences.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, easing discomfort and pain associated with gum infections.

Antibacterial properties: Kansas coneflower tea has been shown to possess antibacterial properties that can help combat bacterial growth and inhibit the spread of infection, promoting healing and reducing the risk of further complications.

Wound-healing compounds: The tea contains wound-healing compounds like rosmarinic acid, which can accelerate the healing process by improving blood circulation, enhancing tissue repair, and reducing oxidative stress, all of which are essential for combating gum infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections, making them effective in treating gum infections.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria, making them a valuable addition to medicinal teas.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried echinacea angustifolia flowers and leaves for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the echinacea mixture with boiling water in a teapot or infuser and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth to remove the herb particles.
Drink the tea three times a day, after meals and before bedtime, for at least two weeks to alleviate gum infection symptoms.
Store any leftover tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cloves teas helps with gum infection because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound eugenol in cloves has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause periodontal disease, reducing inflammation and swelling in the gums. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial properties help eliminate biofilms that can harbor harmful bacteria, promoting a healthy oral environment and aiding in the healing process. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe gum pain and reduce infection.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial property: Cloves contain eugenol, which has antimicrobial properties that can help kill bacteria that cause gum infections and reduce inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory property: Eugenol in cloves also exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Analgesic property: The essential oil of cloves contains analgesic compounds that can help alleviate toothache and other gum-related pains, providing relief from discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antimicrobial compounds, clove roots help reduce bacterial growth and inflammation associated with gum infections.
Rhyzomes: With their unique bioactive compounds, clove rhyzomes have antiseptic properties that aid in preventing the spread of infection and promoting healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh syzygium aromaticum leaves and flowers or purchase dried forms in suitable quantities for brewing.
Measure one teaspoon of dried whole cloves or one tablespoon of loose-leaf clove mixture into a heat-resistant teapot or infuser.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the clove ingredients and let it steep for 5 to 7 minutes, allowing flavors to mingle.
Strain the tea carefully through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding any solids left behind.
Enjoy the warm, aromatic tea immediately, sipping slowly as needed throughout the day or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with gum infection because it is rich in antibacterial properties that effectively combat against the bacteria responsible for periodontal disease. The tea's flavonoids and saponins work together to reduce inflammation, swelling, and pain in the gums, while its antimicrobial compounds inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause infection. Additionally, pot marigold teas' anti-inflammatory properties help to promote healthy gum tissue and reduce bleeding, making it a natural remedy for gum infections and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which helps combat gum infections caused by these microorganisms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in pot marigold tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation and infection.
Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold tea is rich in antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals, which can damage gum tissues and contribute to infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, the roots help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with gum infections.
Barks: The barks contain tannins, which have antiseptic properties that can help to combat bacterial growth and prevent further infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers in the morning for optimal potency and antimicrobial properties.
Rinse 1-2 teaspoons of dried or fresh calendula flowers thoroughly with filtered water to remove any impurities.
Steep the calendula flowers in a teapot or infuser with 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release its bioactive compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.
Allow the tea to cool for at least 30 minutes before gargling and swishing it in your mouth for 1-2 minutes to help combat gum infection.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen teas helps with gum infection because of its antibacterial properties, which target the bacteria that cause inflammation and swelling in the gums. The tea's methyl salicylate content also provides anti-inflammatory relief, reducing redness and pain associated with gum infections. Additionally, wintergreen's antiseptic qualities help to combat the bacterial biofilm that forms on teeth and gums, promoting a healthy oral environment and speeding up the healing process. As a result, regular consumption of herbal wintergreen tea can effectively alleviate symptoms of gum infection and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a main component of wintergreen oil, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling in the gums, making it effective in soothing gum infections.

Antimicrobial properties: Wintergreen tea contains compounds like methyl salicylate, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, such as Streptococcus mutans, helping to prevent further infection and promote healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in wintergreen tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with gum infections, allowing the gums to heal faster.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wintergreen roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in gum infections.
Barks: Wintergreen barks contain salicylic acid, which has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects that can aid in treating gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh wintergreen leaves or 1 ounce dried wintergreen leaves.
Combine the harvested wintergreen leaves with 8 cups of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 15-20 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse their medicinal properties.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, discarding solids.
Drink 1 cup of the cooled tea three times daily for 7-10 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Danshen teas helps with gum infection because of its antibacterial properties, which target the bacteria responsible for the infection. The tea's active compounds, such as salvianolic acid and danshensu, have been shown to inhibit the growth of pathogens, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the gums. Additionally, danshen tea has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with gum infections, providing relief from symptoms like pain and bleeding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Danshen tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as salvianolic acids, help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum infections.

Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal compounds present in danshen tea, like tanshinones, inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause gum infections, preventing their spread and promoting healing.
Vasodilatory properties: Danshen tea's vasodilating effects help improve blood flow to the affected area, which aids in delivering oxygen and nutrients necessary for wound healing and tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help combat gum infections.
Rhyzomes: Contain a range of sesquiterpenes, including Danshensu, which has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities against gum infection-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Rinse dried danshen roots with cold running water to remove any impurities.
Measure out one teaspoon of the rinsed roots and combine it with eight ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes in a teapot or infuser to allow the herbs to release their active compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any solids.
Allow the tea to cool down slightly before drinking it three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with gum infection because of its antimicrobial properties, which combat bacteria that cause inflammation and discomfort. The menthol in peppermint also has a numbing effect, reducing pain and swelling associated with gum infections. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce redness and bleeding, promoting healing and soothing irritated gums. By drinking herbal peppermint teas regularly, individuals can alleviate symptoms of gum infection and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Peppermint tea's antibacterial properties help combat the growth of bacteria that cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint tea reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, making it easier to manage symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in peppermint tea help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of gum disease.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing menthol and other bioactive compounds, roots of peppermint plant are effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with gum infections.
Rhyzomes: Having anti-inflammatory properties, rhyzomes of peppermint plant help to alleviate swelling and redness caused by gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves or purchase dried peppermint herb for best flavor.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried peppermint herb or two tablespoons of fresh leaves per cup.
Boil water in a teapot or kettle for seven to ten minutes and let it cool slightly.
Add the measured peppermint herb or leaves to the boiling water and steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids then drink hot or iced within an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram teas helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which combat the bacteria that cause periodontal disease. The tea's high concentration of rosmarinic acid and camphor helps to reduce inflammation and eliminate bad breath. Additionally, wild marjoram's antiseptic compounds work to prevent further bacterial growth, promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing the risk of infection. As a natural remedy, wild marjoram tea provides an effective alternative to traditional treatments for gum infection.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wild marjoram's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to combat bacterial infections in the gums, reducing inflammation and preventing further infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in wild marjoram tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections and promote healing.

Antioxidant properties: Wild marjoram's antioxidants help to protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall oral health and reducing the risk of chronic diseases.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of wild marjoram contain antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial and fungal infections in the gums.
Seeds: Wild marjoram seeds have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh wild marjoram leaves in the early morning and rinse them gently with cool water.
Combine one teaspoon of dried wild marjoram or three teaspoons of fresh wild marjoram leaves with one cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for ten to fifteen minutes, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool down to a comfortable drinking temperature and sip it slowly throughout the day.
Repeat this process twice daily for up to two weeks or as recommended by your healthcare provider.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with gum infection because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which target the bacteria responsible for causing periodontal disease. The tea's active compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, penetrate deep into the gums, killing off harmful microorganisms and reducing inflammation. As a result, herbal thyme teas can help alleviate symptoms of gum infection, including bleeding, swelling, and bad breath, promoting healthy gums and a strong oral environment.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria that cause gum infections, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol, reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections, making it easier to manage symptoms.
Antioxidant properties: Thyme's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate gum infections, protecting the gums and surrounding tissues from further damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are used to create a tea that helps reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, making them effective against gum infections.
Barks: Thyme barks contain compounds that have antibacterial properties, which can help combat infection-causing bacteria in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves or purchase dried thyme flowers and measure out two teaspoons for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the thyme and boiling water in a heat-resistant teapot and allow it to steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the solids.
Add one teaspoon of honey to the tea and stir well to dissolve, if desired, for a soothing flavor.
Drink the tea three times daily after meals and before bed for at least two weeks to aid in gum infection treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Radium weed teas helps with gum infection because of its natural antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as chamazulene and luteolin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause gum inflammation and infection. Additionally, radium weed tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections. By drinking radium weed tea regularly, individuals can potentially prevent or alleviate symptoms of gum disease, promoting healthy gums and a strong oral microbiome.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the radium weed plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum infections.
Barks: The barks of the radium weed plant have antibacterial properties, which help combat the bacteria that cause gum infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried euphorbia peplus leaves and stems according to your supplier's instructions.
Rinse the plant material with pure water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steep one teaspoon of dried euphorbia peplus in boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Store the tea in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Many herbs have antibacterial compounds that can help combat bacterial infections in the gums, such as tea tree oil or chamomile.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like turmeric, ginger, and boswellia have anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Antioxidant properties: Antioxidants in herbs like green tea, peppermint, and sage can help protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by inflammation.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for gum infection are a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms of gingivitis and periodontitis. These teas work by reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and promoting healing in the gums. For example, tea tree oil-infused tea has antiseptic properties that combat bacterial growth, while chamomile tea reduces swelling and soothes irritated gums. Drinking these teas regularly can improve oral health, reduce pain and discomfort, and restore confidence in one's smile.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gum infection with herbal teas is to steep dried sage leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain and drink as a warm tea three times a day. Another option is to mix equal parts of chamomile and calendula flowers, steeping them in hot water for 10-15 minutes. This tea helps reduce inflammation and promotes healing.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum infection that are treated with herbal teas are periodontal diseases, toothaches, and mouth sores. Teas made from herbs like sage, chamomile, and calendula have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and soothe the gums. Ginger tea is also used to alleviate pain and inflammation associated with toothaches and mouth sores, while peppermint tea helps freshen breath and reduce bad odors.
